ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user on the social media platform */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "friends_count" INTEGER /* Number of friends the user has */,
  "favourites_count" INTEGER /* Number of favourites the user has */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user is verified */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "last_active" TEXT /* Date when the user was last active */,
  "user_type" TEXT /* Type of user (e.g., individual, brand, influencer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "posted_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the tweet was posted */,
  "text" TEXT /* Original text of the tweet */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Hashtags used in the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for iPhone) */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the tweet is a retweet */,
  "clean_hashtags" TEXT /* Cleaned and normalized hashtags */,
  "clean_text" TEXT /* Cleaned and normalized text */,
  "clean_tweets" TEXT /* Processed and normalized tweet text for analysis */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "media_count" INTEGER /* Number of media attachments in the tweet */,
  "tweet_length" INTEGER /* Length of the tweet text */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the metric relates to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., followers, friends, favourites) */,
  "metric_value" INTEGER /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., API, web scrape) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_metrics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the metric relates to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., retweets, likes, shares) */,
  "metric_value" INTEGER /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the metric (e.g., API, web scrape) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_metrics_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content analysis */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet analyzed */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag used in the tweet */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the tweet is a retweet */,
  "clean_hashtag" TEXT /* Cleaned and normalized hashtag */,
  "clean_text" TEXT /* Cleaned and normalized text */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment analysis score of the tweet */,
  "topic_category" TEXT /* Category of the topic discussed in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_analysis_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trending_topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trending topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the trending topic */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the trend */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the trend */,
  "tweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of tweets related to the topic */,
  "peak_date" TEXT /* Date when the topic reached its peak popularity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the topic predicted */,
  "predicted_date" TEXT /* Date the prediction is for */,
  "predicted_tweet_count" INTEGER /* Predicted number of tweets related to the topic */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_topic_id FOREIGN KEY ("topic_id") REFERENCES trending_topics ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the report is for */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet the report is about */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., engagement, content) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, HTML) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the compliance record relates to */,
  "compliance_type" TEXT /* Type of compliance (e.g., GDPR, CCPA) */,
  "compliance_date" TEXT /* Date the compliance was checked */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of compliance (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the compliance status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security" (
  "security_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each security measure */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the security measure relates to */,
  "security_type" TEXT /* Type of security measure (e.g., encryption, authentication) */,
  "security_date" TEXT /* Date the security measure was implemented */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the security measure (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the security measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("security_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_security_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
